title: carex straminea var. mirabilis
intro: Carex straminea var. mirabilis, also known as the lesser quaking grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
